notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: A daily close back above the SMA50 (~$301) on above-average volume would confirm the bottom and push toward a more aggressive buy; conversely a decisive break below the $260 52w low on volume would flip this to neutral/trim as trend continuation.

positional.falsifier: Next earnings showing book-to-bill >1.05 and backlog YoY expansion with op margin recovering toward 13%+ would validate the deep-buy and justify holding conviction; conversely B/B <0.95 with negative backlog YoY and continued margin compression would flip this to a hold/trim as the thesis breaks.

strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E confirmed above 23x AND >115% of 3Y average with no margin-recovery path would flip this to a clear trim; conversely a lifted FY27 NDAA topline / major multi-billion program award with margin guidance recovery toward 14% would flip it to a strategic buy.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp multi-day break below the SMA50 ($1712) on heavy volume would flip this to a trim/negative-momentum tactical read.

positional.falsifier: A drawdown >15% from highs (toward ~$1480) would flip positional to a genuine BUY per the archetype playbook.

strategic.falsifier: Book-to-bill dropping below 1.0 combined with TSMC/Samsung/Intel all guiding capex cuts would confirm strategic overvaluation and push this decisively positive (toward +25).

tactical.falsifier: A break of RSI below ~35 with the QUAL factor flipping negative vs SPY would confirm the quality bid has left the name and flip the tactical sign to trim.

positional.falsifier: A decisive close below the 200-day SMA ($471.83) on rising volume, breaking the golden-cross structure, would flip the positional sign to trim.

strategic.falsifier: Two consecutive quarters of negative like-for-like price/mix ex-FX AND declining backlog would confirm genuine moat erosion and flip this to a clear sell; conversely a confirmed >$1B H2/semi project win with backlog acceleration would flip it to a buy.

tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking below 40 with a fresh 52w low under $349 on rising volume would confirm a downtrend and flip tactical to neutral/trim; conversely an RSI reclaim of 60 confirms the buy.

positional.falsifier: Price reclaiming the 50-day SMA ($401.73) and starting to close the MA gap would confirm the buy; conversely a decisive break below $349 with expanding downside would flip positional toward neutral.

strategic.falsifier: Azure constant-currency growth decelerating below 22% for two consecutive quarters, or an OpenAI partnership unwind/adverse renegotiation, would break the strategic thesis and flip the score toward neutral/trim.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp 10Y spike (>25bps in days) triggering a sympathetic 3-4% ENB drop would flip this to a tactical buy.

positional.falsifier: 10Y yield falling meaningfully (toward ~4.2%) or a dividend-growth guidance raise widening the yield spread back above ~150bps would flip this negative (buy).

strategic.falsifier: A confirmed easing cycle (multiple Fed/BoC cuts) or a large accretive secured-project addition lifting the growth outlook would flip strategic to a buy.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive daily close back above the 50-day ($14.92) on rising volume would flip this to a buy; conversely a break below ~$13.50 would confirm the trim.

positional.falsifier: GLNCY losing the 200-day ($12.73) alongside widening HY OAS above ~3.5% would flip this positive; sustained copper breakout above ~$4.50/lb with GLNCY reclaiming the 50-day would deepen the buy.

strategic.falsifier: Trailing P/E collapsing toward single digits (earnings spiking to a cyclical PEAK) plus copper at multi-year highs would flip this to a trim; alternatively a confirmed China demand collapse with LME inventories surging would neutralize the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive reclaim of the 50DMA ($38.86) on rising ETF inflows would flip this to tactical buy; conversely a clean break below $32.84 on heavy volume would flip it to sell.

positional.falsifier: ETF flows turning sustainably positive AND price reclaiming the 50DMA would validate max buy; conversely, continued net ETF outflows with a break below the 52w low $32.84 would flip me to neutral/sell as the cycle-break thesis confirms.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained breakdown in ETF AUM (persistent multi-month net redemptions) plus a macro regime of rising real rates would flip strategic to negative-outlook (sell); a Fed pivot to cuts plus accelerating institutional inflows would push it to strong strategic buy.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close below the 200-day SMA (~$98.88) on rising volume, or a sharp MXN move beyond 18.0, would flip this to a positive (trim) tactical read.

positional.falsifier: A confirmed downtrend (close below 200-day) combined with a Q2 volume/margin miss would flip positional to positive; conversely reclaiming the 50-day would deepen the buy.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained MXN break above ~18.5 (peso regime shift) or a KO-parent LatAm guidance cut would flip strategic to neutral/positive; a firming peso below 17.0 plus a Banxico soft-landing pivot would deepen the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close above the 50-day ($18.29) or a sustained break below the 200-day ($15.91) would flip the tactical sign.

positional.falsifier: An official cut to the dividend payout policy or a mandated large capex reallocation away from shareholder returns would flip this positive (trim).

strategic.falsifier: Concrete government action mandating below-market fuel pricing or diverting the dividend, OR a WTI break below ~$60 undermining payout coverage, would flip this to a trim.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained freight rate collapse driving Maersk into negative free cash flow with a dividend/buyback cut, or Logistics EBIT margin deterioration below ~2%, would remove the value case and flip strategic toward positive.

tactical.falsifier: A break back below SMA50 ($482.77) on rising volume, or RSI rolling over from overbought while XBI simultaneously turns down, would flip this to trim.

positional.falsifier: XBI 90d rolling negative (freeze re-establishing) or TMO losing the QUAL bid and dropping below SMA50 would flip the positional sign to trim.

strategic.falsifier: A guide-down / organic growth staying stuck at ~1% with margin deterioration, or simultaneous China capex collapse + NIH funding cuts, would flip strategic to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A daily close back above the 50-day SMA ($103.05) on expanding volume with RSI reclaiming 55 would confirm the bottom is in and validate the buy; conversely a break below the 52w low of $81.24 would flip me to neutral/trim as the drawdown becomes a structural repricing rather than a dip.

positional.falsifier: A reversal of the death cross (50-day reclaiming the 200-day trajectory) alongside IGV flipping to lead SPY over 30d would confirm the positional buy; sustained negative EPS revisions or a break of the 52w low would flip me to neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: A cRPO acceleration back above ~20% with Now Assist Pro Plus revenue contribution inflecting higher would flip this decisively negative (strategic buy); confirmed evidence of Agentforce/Copilot displacing ServiceNow seats or cRPO decelerating below 18% would flip it to a firmer positive trim.

tactical.falsifier: A drawdown deepening past 12-15% from the 52w high (below ~$530 sustained) would flip this to a tactical buy; conversely a break to new highs on accelerating volume would confirm continuation not exhaustion.

positional.falsifier: Cross-border volume printing <6% or rebates/incentives outpacing gross revenue growth for a second consecutive quarter would flip this positive (trim); a clean beat-and-raise with cross-border >12% confirms the buy.

strategic.falsifier: US interchange/network-fee legislation reaching a floor vote OR credible data showing stablecoins displacing card volume at real-economy merchants would flip this positive; sustained cross-border <6% would too.

tactical.falsifier: If the Q2 print (now public) showed procedure growth below 12% WITH competitor share-loss evidence, or the stock reverses back below $380 (new lows), the tactical buy flips to neutral/trim.

positional.falsifier: Evidence of measurable US procedure-share loss to Hugo/Ottava, or EPS revisions turning sharply negative, would flip this from buy toward neutral/trim regardless of price level.
